design_philosophy.core_loop: Trap enemies in bubbles by blowing them from your mouth, pop bubbles to defeat enemies and collect bonus items. Progress through levels of increasing difficulty.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing bubble trajectory and enemy positioning while avoiding direct contact; time pressure as levels become more complex
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a dragon using bubble-blowing as a unique combat mechanic to rescue girlfriends from a monster-filled cave
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes two-player cooperative gameplay as the intended experience (insertable second coin); 100 levels of systematic difficulty escalation; bubble mechanics as core identity rather than traditional platformer jumping combat

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with password system in NES version for level selection; FDS version uses save function
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system allows players to select starting level, providing agency to skip early content or replay specific difficulty tiers

progression.unlock_mechanism: 100 sequential levels, each progressively more difficult; enemies and level layouts become increasingly complex
replayability_hooks: Two-player cooperative gameplay encouraged as primary mode
Bonus items dropped by enemies reward skilled play and exploration
Password system enables practice on specific difficulty spikes
High arcade pedigree suggests score-chasing and speedrun optimization potential

world_building_techniques: Minimal intro text: 'Now it is the Beginning of a Fantastic Story!' establishes rescue mission premise
Setting name: 'Cave of Monsters' suggests hostile, dangerous environment
Character design: Bub (green) and Bob (blue) dragons with girlfriend rescue motivation as thin narrative wrapper

faction_indicators: Two-player dragon brothers (Bub and Bob) suggest cooperative alliance
Rescue mission framing (saving girlfriends) indicates heroic faction alignment
danger_signals: Cave of Monsters setting as hostile territory
Progressive enemy complexity suggests escalating threat
environmental_storytelling: Level progression through cave system implies deeper penetration into monster territory
Enemy variety and placement suggests ecological hierarchy within cave
Bonus items as environmental rewards suggest exploration value within levels
